own_review.issue: System.exit(-1) in the scanFluo FluoException catch executes inside the outer try-with-resources body, so FluoClient.close() is never invoked on scan failure (ZK/client resources not closed cleanly before JVM death). Minor since the JVM exits anyway, but exit-inside-twr is a known footgun.

Removal of entriesFound counter and the 'No data found' message makes an empty scan produce zero output; user cannot distinguish 'no matching data' from a no-op. Arguably intentional per issue #981 (stop polluting stdout with meta info), but it is a user-facing behavior change beyond pure meta-info removal and worth calling out.

The System.err + System.exit(-1) change is an unrelated bug fix bundled into a 'stop printing meta info' PR (scope mixing); previously a failed scan exited 0. The fix itself is correct and matches the existing IllegalArgumentException handlers (lines 106-107/158-159).
exit(-1) wraps to status 255; conventional CLI failure code is 1. Pre-existing pattern in this file, trivial.
Stray blank lines left at the top of scanFluo/scanAccumulo after deleting the println blocks; style leftover.

points.point: err+exit for FluoException consistent with FluoCommand/BaseOpts/FluoExec patterns (cited lines verified accurate)
scanAccumulo same change; verified both callers (FluoScan.java:105/107, AppRunner.java:76/78) call without try-catch so exit is appropriate for CLI

CommandUtil.java:28-30,38-40 still prints errors to stdout - follow-up suggestion (verified true), but the claim it is 'the only file in the CLI layer that prints errors to stdout' is false (FluoList, FluoInit:191, ValidateAppName, ClusterUtil also do)
ScanTest covers only getSpan/getColumns; removed 'No data found' and new exit(-1) behavior untested (verified)
Observation that IllegalArgumentException handler already used err+exit and PR makes others match - accurate but pure restatement/praise, no actionable content

missed: System.exit(-1) inside try-with-resources skips FluoClient.close() on scan failure
UX regression: empty scan now silent, indistinguishable from no-op (v4 mentioned the removal only as a test-coverage note, never raised the behavior concern)
exceeds: CommandUtil stdout-for-errors consistency follow-up (verified, neither human nor judge found)
ScanTest coverage gap for changed behavior (verified, neither human nor judge found)

points.point: Questions whether PR title means meta info should be RESTORED rather than removed - hedged clarifying question, but diff+issue intent are clear (human confirms removal was the stated goal); noise, not fabrication
err vs out switch consistent with FluoWorkerImpl 87/94, FluoOracleImpl 90/97 and ScanUtil's own 106/158 (all line refs verified accurate)
exit(-1) consistent with existing handlers; verified callers FluoScan.main/AppRunner.scan have no code after the call and no library consumers in-repo affected (all verified)
Same for scanAccumulo - one-line duplicate of previous point with no new analysis
Removal of entriesFound/'No data found' makes empty scans silent; cannot distinguish zero results from silent failure - real behavior regression, matches judge finding
Removal of 'Scanning snapshot...' startup feedback - but removing exactly that message IS the PR's stated purpose per issue #981; mild noise, real fact

missed: System.exit(-1) inside try-with-resources skips FluoClient.close() on scan failure

qualitative: Tiny CLI-cleanup PR where the single human point (the err/exit change is an unrelated exit-code bug fix) was substantively engaged by both candidates. v4 shines on cross-codebase verification and produces two genuine exceeds (CommandUtil stdout inconsistency, test-coverage gap), but overclaims with a false 'only file in the CLI layer prints errors to stdout' assertion (FluoList, FluoInit, ValidateAppName, ClusterUtil all do) and never raises the empty-scan UX regression it tangentially acknowledged. v5 catches that UX regression cleanly with zero fabrications and accurate line references throughout, but burns points on a confused intent question and a duplicate one-liner. Both missed that System.exit(-1) inside the try-with-resources skips FluoClient.close(). Net result is a tie at +2, with v5 the more trustworthy text (no fabrications) and v4 the more exploratory one.
